prose_fallback.decision_handoff_excerpt:
# Decision summary

BTC finishing above $70,000 on the Apr 20 noon-ET Binance 1-minute close remains the base case, but the swarm’s mild bearishness versus the 0.87 market is directionally right: current spot around $74.0k-$74.1k gives a real cushion, yet the contract still concentrates all risk into one exact future minute close, so I would synthesize a modestly-below-market Yes range rather than endorse the market price outright.

## Why this may matter now

Market implies 0.87 Yes; my synthesized range is 0.80-0.85 Yes. That is a marginal-to-moderate bearish edge versus market, not a directional reversal. The likely mispricing is that traders may be treating current in-the-money status as closer to a touch market than this exact future 1-minute close contract warrants.

## Shift versus swarm baseline

The provisional swarm center was about 0.82, and I stay close to it. I moved slightly upward in range shape versus the most bearish lane because fresh synthesis-stage checks confirmed BTC still sits around 74.0k-74.1k on Binance and recent daily context remains supportive. I did not move up to market because that verification did not solve the actual remaining uncertainty: five mor …

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: The market is directionally right that BTC is likely to finish above 70000 on Apr 20, but the 0.87 price looks mildly rich relative to remaining five-day volatility into one exact Binance minute close.
main_logical_chain: Start from the market's 0.87 price as an information-rich prior.
Verify the governing source and confirm BTC is currently around 74000 on Binance.
Check one independent contextual source and confirm the broader market also sees BTC around 74000.
Apply a modest discount because the contract settles on one future minute close several days away.
strongest_supports: Binance BTCUSDT traded around 73994.6 during the run, well above 70000.
CoinGecko independently showed BTC around 74006 with the sampled 1-day range above 70000.
strongest_disconfirmers: Settlement depends on one future Binance 12:00 ET minute close, not today's spot.
BTC can move more than 5% over five days, so a sub-70000 close remains plausible.
unresolved_ambiguities: How stable the current 74000 regime remains through Apr 20 noon ET.
Whether any near-term catalyst or shock emerges before settlement.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A sustained Binance move back toward or below 70000, or new evidence of exchange-specific/resolution-surface issues, would push the estimate lower.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: High: the market resolves at an exact future minute on 2026-04-20 12:00 ET, so remaining path volatility matters.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality direct governing-source check plus one medium-high-quality contextual verification; source-of-truth ambiguity is fairly low but not zero.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: Yes is favored because BTC is materially above $70k, but the market likely overprices certainty because settlement depends on one exact future Binance BTC/USDT 1-minute close at noon ET rather than any prior touch.
main_logical_chain: Verify the governing source and exact contract mechanics: Binance BTC/USDT, 1-minute candle, April 20 12:00 ET, final Close > $70,000.
Check current Binance price surface and confirm BTC is materially above threshold several days early.
Treat current cushion as strong support for Yes but not near-certainty because the contract is close-specific rather than touch-based.
Mark probability below market because a single downside move at the exact observation minute can still resolve No.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance spot checks show BTCUSDT around $73,995, comfortably above the $70,000 threshold.
There are only about five days left, so maintaining a multi-thousand-dollar cushion is more likely than not absent a sharp selloff.
strongest_disconfirmers: The current cushion itself may justify a probability closer to the market's 0.87 than to 0.79.
Ordinary BTC volatility may still leave the exact April 20 noon ET close above $70k more often than this variant estimate assumes.
unresolved_ambiguities: The qualifying candle has not occurred yet, so the final governing-source proof cannot yet be captured.
UI candle labeling/time-conversion operational details are probably straightforward but still worth care at rerun near resolution.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: I would move higher if BTC retains or expands its cushion into resolution; I would move lower if BTC falls back toward $70k or new risk events raise downside odds.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: Highly date-sensitive: the market resolves on the exact April 20 noon ET 1-minute close, not on current spot or any earlier touch.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: High-quality direct governing source verification, but evidence independence is only medium-low because the main sources are mechanically linked to the same settlement surface.

selected_persona_sidecars.main_thesis: BTC is already materially above 70000 on Binance, making Yes likely by April 20 noon ET, but exact-minute close mechanics keep real downside risk alive.
main_logical_chain: Verify the governing contract mechanics directly from the market rules.
Verify current Binance BTCUSDT is materially above 70000.
Treat recent Binance daily closes as contextual evidence that BTC is operating above the threshold regime.
Discount from near-certainty because this is an exact-minute close market with five days of residual volatility.
strongest_supports: Direct Binance ticker check showed BTCUSDT around 73974, about 5.7% above the threshold.
Recent Binance daily closes were also above 70000, suggesting the setup is not just a transient spike.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract resolves on one exact 12:00 ET one-minute close, not on any touch or daily close.
A normal crypto drawdown over five days could still push BTC below 70000 at the qualifying minute.
unresolved_ambiguities: Final candle-proof cannot yet be captured because the event has not occurred.
UI-specific Binance candle display nuances remain a minor source-of-truth handling risk.
selected_persona_sidecars.what_would_change_view: A sustained BTC move back toward or below 70000 on Binance before April 20 would move the estimate down materially; continued holding well above low-70k into April 18-19 would move it up somewhat.
selected_persona_sidecars.timing_relevance: The event resolves in about five days and depends on one exact noon ET minute close, so short-horizon volatility remains materially relevant.
selected_persona_sidecars.source_quality_view: Primary rules source and governing venue check are strong, but evidence independence is only medium-low because the contract appropriately centers on Binance.
